front: Novel: two neighboring islands war over the correct way to crack open an egg; one's fleet is destroyed by the protagonist
Novel: a race of rational talking horses contrasts with the bestial Yahoos
Novel: the Academy of Projectors in Lagado contains a computer-like device called 'The Engine'; residents of a floating island need 'flappers' to stay focused
Essay: suggests 'lessening the number of papists' and making 'admirable gloves for ladies' as benefits of eating Irish children
Essay: cites 'the famous Psalmanazar, a native of the island of Formosa'; ridicules the economist William Petty
Work: three brothers (Peter, Martin, Jack) inherit coats from their father representing Catholicism, Anglicanism, and Calvinism; Martin alone retains his original coat
back: A Tale of a Tub (Jonathan Swift)

front: Work: contains a 'Digression on Madness' and discusses the Aeolists, who believe learning happens through wind
back: A Tale of a Tub (Jonathan Swift)

front: Work: a bee destroys a spider's web; Aesop judges the dispute, yielding the phrase 'sweetness and light' later borrowed by Matthew Arnold
back: The Battle of the Books (Jonathan Swift)

front: Work: the goddess Criticism lives atop 'a snowy Mountain in Nova Zembla'; Momus recruits her after Jupiter consults the book of fate
back: The Battle of the Books (Jonathan Swift)

front: Poem: Strephon sneaks into the title location and discovers 'Oh! Celia, Celia, Celia shits!'; he is advised to see women as 'Tulips rais'd from Dung'
Author: used the pseudonym Isaac Bickerstaff to predict the death of astrologer John Partridge; Richard Steele later adopted the name for The Tatler's fictional editor
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works

front: Author: the Drapier's Letters attacked William Wood's copper coinage scheme in Ireland
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works

front: Author: coined the name 'Vanessa' from lover Esther Vanhomrigh; the poem 'Cadenus and Vanessa' anagrams his profession (Dean)
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works

front: Author: 'Thoughts on Various Subjects, Moral and Diverting' contains 'just enough religion to make us hate' and provided the title for A Confederacy of Dunces
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works

front: Author: Latin epitaph mentions 'saeva Indignatio' (savage indignation); translated by W.B. Yeats, who called him a 'World-Besotted Traveler'
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works

front: Poem: 'Triumphant Tories, and desponding Whigs, forget their feuds and join to save their wigs'; features drowned puppies, dead cats, and turnip-tops flowing downhill
back: A Description of a City Shower (Jonathan Swift)
work: Isaac Bickerstaff / Drapier's Letters / Other Works
